mechanism_narrative: Persistent high unemployment signals weak consumer demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. A continuously elevated unemployment rate reflects a struggling economy, dampening investor sentiment and equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is currently 9.0% as of April 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE is above its 12-month moving average, or the average return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer spending, which directly impacts corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations are revised downwards.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS has been declining recently, with April 2009 value at 324323.0
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met, or the average return is not statistically different from zero in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is at a historically low level and unemployment is simultaneously rising, it signals a desperate monetary policy response to a deteriorating labor market, indicating a severe economic downturn and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 0.15% and UNRATE is 9.0% as of April 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and UNRATE is above 8.0%, or the interaction term between low rates and high unemployment shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In a period of economic recovery, a significant month-over-month increase in housing starts indicates renewed confidence and investment in the economy, which typically precedes broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 505.0 in March 2009, indicating low base for potential rebound
failure_condition: During identified recovery periods, a positive 5% monthly change in HOUST does not lead to positive SPX 5-day returns in at least 60% of cases, or the average return is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained contraction in industrial production signals a significant slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic activity, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is 85.9344 in April 2009, indicating a severe contraction
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of instances where INDPRO shows a severe contraction, or the average return is not significantly negative in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates weak aggregate demand and potential deflationary pressures, which can negatively impact corporate pricing power and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL is 212.709 in April 2009, indicating low inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when CPIAUCSL 12-month growth is below 1.0%, or the correlation between low inflation and returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When employment (PAYEMS) is declining while retail sales (RSAFS) are also falling, it indicates a broad-based weakening of both the labor market and consumer demand, signaling immediate negative pressure on S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Both PAYEMS and RSAFS have been declining in recent months (April 2009 values)
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where both PAYEMS and RSAFS show monthly declines, or the interaction effect between these two series is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in GDP growth after a period of contraction signals an economic turning point and improved corporate prospects, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as investor confidence returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth turns positive after two consecutive negative quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 11340.9 in Q1 2009 (vintage), indicating recent contraction
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances following a positive GDPC1 growth after two negative quarters, or the average return is not statistically positive in such periods.
